headlines.title: Chinese Refiners Slash Crude Runs to Lowest Level Since 2022
headlines.summary: Chinese refiners slashed their crude runs to the lowest level since August 2022 as China has reduced refinery utilization and slashed crude imports as oil prices soar amid the Iran war. Crude throughput at Chinese refineries slumped by 5.8% in April from a year earlier, to about

headlines.title: HSBC Bets $4 Billion on China’s Clean Energy Export Boom

headlines.title: The Middle East Oil Crisis Has Already Cost Global Businesses $25 Billion
headlines.summary: The oil and gas crunch caused by the war between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, has cost global businesses $25 billion, Reuters reported today, adding that the bill is going to climb higher still. headlines.title: Brent Breaks $111 as Oil Markets Brace for Shortages
headlines.summary: Oil prices climbed in early Asian trade on Monday as drone attacks on both the UAE and Saudi Arabia further dimmed hopes of any de-escalation in the region. headlines.title: Oil Shortage Scenario Looms Large
headlines.summary: The world is running out of oil. Implausible three months ago, the likelihood of a crude shortage on a global scale is becoming increasingly realistic with each day that the Strait of Hormuz remains almost completely blocked. headlines.title: Clean Energy’s Dirty Secret Lies in Critical Mineral Extraction
